name: Cox's Bazar Beach
about: Cox's Bazar is the world's longest natural sea beach stretching over 120 kilometers along the Bay of Bengal. Visitors enjoy dramatic sunsets, fresh seafood, the famous marine drive, and exciting coastal adventures throughout the year.

name: Saint Martin Island
about: Saint Martin Island is the only coral island in Bangladesh, known for its turquoise water, coconut palm groves, and peaceful beaches. Snorkeling around the coral reef at low tide reveals a vibrant underwater world perfect for sea lovers.

name: Sundarbans
about: The Sundarbans is the largest mangrove forest in the world and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, home to the Royal Bengal Tiger, spotted deer, and hundreds of bird species. Boat cruises weave through labyrinthine tidal rivers and dense mangrove canopy.

name: Sajek Valley
about: Perched at 1,800 ft in the Chittagong Hill Tracts, Sajek Valley greets visitors with rolling clouds, indigenous Chakma and Lusai culture, and unforgettable sunrise views above the mist-filled valleys. It is one of the most breathtaking destinations in Bangladesh.

name: Tanguar Haor
about: Tanguar Haor is a unique Ramsar wetland ecosystem in Sunamganj famous for boat travel, migratory birds, and crystal-clear water during monsoon season. Vast open water reflecting the sky makes it one of the most relaxing nature escapes in Bangladesh.

name: Ratargul Swamp Forest
about: Ratargul is the only freshwater swamp forest in Bangladesh, located in Sylhet. During the rainy season the entire forest submerges and visitors glide through by wooden boat among emerald-green canopy and ghostly submerged tree trunks.

name: Jaflong
about: Jaflong is situated on the India border in Sylhet, famous for its crystal-clear Piyain river and scenic stone-collection beds. The surrounding Khasia tribal hills create stunning layered scenery that attracts thousands of tourists every year.

name: Tea Gardens Sylhet
about: Rolling carpets of bright green tea bushes stretch endlessly across the Sylhet landscape. Nature walks through the gardens reveal workers hand-picking leaves and the sweet scent of fresh tea fills the peaceful surrounding air.

name: Bandarban Hill
about: Bandarban is the gateway hill district offering lush green mountain ranges, peaceful tribal villages, and serene rivers. It serves as the base for trekkers heading to Bangladesh's highest and most remote Himalayan foothills.

name: Nilachal Bandarban
about: Known as the Darjeeling of Bangladesh, Nilachal hilltop sits above the clouds at dawn. The panoramic view of Bandarban town wrapped in morning mist with mountain ranges stretching in every direction is an unforgettable sight for visitors.

name: Nafakhum Waterfall
about: Nafakhum is considered one of the most powerful and scenic waterfalls in Bangladesh. The adventurous multi-day trek through hills, rivers, and remote Bawm tribal villages to reach Nafakhum is itself a legendary journey worth every step.

name: Boga Lake
about: Boga Lake is a natural lake of volcanic origin nestled high in the Bandarban hills at 1,246 ft altitude. Adventure lovers trek through mountain paths to enjoy the serene turquoise lake surrounded by dense forest and indigenous tribal culture.

name: Debotakhum
about: Debotakhum is a mysterious narrow canyon carved through rock by the Sangu river in Bandarban. Visitors wade through cold chest-deep water and travel by boat through pitch-dark tunnels to reach a hidden chamber — a truly thrilling natural adventure.

name: Keokradong Hill
about: Once considered the highest peak of Bangladesh, Keokradong stands at 1,230 m in the Bandarban range. The summit offers sweeping panoramic views across the border hill ranges stretching into Myanmar, rewarding trekkers with an extraordinary vista.

name: Nilgiri Hill
about: Nilgiri at 2,200 ft is often wrapped in thick clouds and mist throughout the year. The hilltop resort gives visitors the rare sensation of sleeping above the clouds with misty mountain ranges visible in every direction at sunrise.

name: Patenga Sea Beach
about: Patenga Sea Beach in Chattogram is popular for breezy evening sea views, street food, and nearby naval base attractions. It is one of the most visited urban beaches in Bangladesh offering easy access from the city center.

name: Himchari
about: Himchari National Park sits just south of Cox's Bazar. A seasonal waterfall cascades down forested green hills directly to the beach below, creating a striking contrast of dense jungle meeting the open sea — a unique natural spectacle.

name: Kuakata Sea Beach
about: Kuakata is one of the rare beaches in South Asia where both sunrise and sunset can be witnessed from the same spot. It is also famous for its Buddhist temples, Rakhain tribal heritage, and calm peaceful travel atmosphere.

name: Madhabkunda Waterfall
about: One of the largest and most picturesque waterfalls in Bangladesh, located in Moulvibazar, Sylhet. The 162-ft cascade surrounded by rocky hillsides and tea-garden greenery remains mesmerizing and accessible throughout the year.

name: Hamham Waterfall
about: Hamham is a multi-tiered waterfall hidden deep inside the Rajkandi reserve forest of Moulvibazar. The jungle trek through dense canopy adds to its mystique. The raw natural beauty and remote location make it a favourite for adventure seekers.

name: Bisnakandi
about: Where two rivers meet at the foot of the Meghalaya hills in Sylhet, Bisnakandi dazzles with crystal-clear water rushing over smooth stone beds. The surrounding mountain panorama becomes especially magical and vibrant during the monsoon season.

name: Lalakhal
about: Lalakhal is famous for its striking blue-green river water that flows from the Jaintia hills on the India border through Sylhet. Boat rides past tea gardens and rubber plantations along the river banks provide deeply relaxing scenery.

name: Alutila Cave
about: Alutila natural cave is a 350-metre underground tunnel carved through a hillside in Khagrachari. Visitors wade through a shallow stream in near-total darkness using torches to navigate — a unique and thrilling natural wonder of the Chittagong Hill Tracts.

name: Saka Haphong Hill
about: Located near the Bangladesh–Myanmar border, Saka Haphong is among the highest peaks in the country. The multi-day expedition through remote Bawm tribal villages of the Dalian headman Para of Remakri Mouja is an adventure of a lifetime.

name: Jogi Haphong Hill
about: Jogi Haphong is a significant peak in the Lawmbok Row range of the Chittagong Hill Tracts. Remote, pristine, and demanding, it was first summited in late 2012 and attracts only the most dedicated and experienced hillwalkers in Bangladesh.

name: Lusai Pahar
about: Lusai Pahar borders Mizoram, India, and is historically significant — its flat mountain top was once declared a temporary capital by a separatist group. The sweeping border-range plateau views and dense surrounding forests make it truly extraordinary.

name: Afiakum Jhorna
about: Afiakum is one of the most remote and pristine waterfalls in Bangladesh, requiring a challenging multi-day jungle trek through Bandarban. The reward is a powerful cascade tumbling into a clear pool surrounded by completely untouched wilderness.

name: Zow Tlang Thanci
about: Zow Tlang is located at the Myanmar border in the Mowdok range at an altitude of 1,022 metres. It is considered the second-highest peak in Bangladesh. The border-range trek offers breathtaking cross-border panoramic views across three countries.
